title: DevTools: flutter_test coverage for top-level ExplorationDevToolsExtension (closes the lenny-g4u gap)
description:
**Coverage gap that allowed lenny-g4u to ship.** Existing shell_test.dart and friends pump `ExplorationShell` directly with stub VM-service-URI callbacks — they never pump `ExplorationDevToolsExtension` wrapping the real `DevToolsExtension` widget. So the build-phase ordering bug (parent-build reading `serviceManager.connectedState` before `DevToolsExtension.initState` runs) was invisible to CI.

## What this bead does

Add an integration-style flutter_test that:

1. Constructs the real `ExplorationDevToolsExtension` widget (not the inner `ExplorationShell`).
2. Stubs `devtools_extensions`'s `serviceManager` global with a fake that:
   - Returns a stub serviceUri
   - Exposes a fake `connectedState` ValueListenable
   - Throws (or records) on access **before** `DevToolsExtension.initState` would have run
3. Pumps the widget tree, asserts that initial frame renders without the 'serviceManager has not been initialized' error.
4. Optional bonus: covers reconnect path — drive `connectedState` to fire and assert a fresh manifest probe.

## Constraints / unknowns

`devtools_extensions` makes the `serviceManager` a top-level getter, so faking it requires either dependency injection (push a configurable getter through the package; PR upstream) or wrapping the production widget tree so the test doesn't go through DevToolsExtension at all (but then we're back to the same gap that allowed g4u).

If neither path is tractable in a single bead, ship a smaller win: a unit test on a new `MainExtensionRoot` widget that owns the `Builder`-wrapping pattern and is testable in isolation. The widget tree in main.dart is then a one-liner that uses `MainExtensionRoot`.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A test exists that would have failed against lenny-g4u's broken main.dart (direct property read above DevToolsExtension)
- [ ] The test passes against the current fixed main.dart
- [ ] If a real DevToolsExtension fake proves infeasible in this bead, the smaller-win factoring of main.dart into a testable widget is acceptable, with a note explaining the punt

title: DevTools: standalone web debug launch + workflow doc for iterating on the panel
description:
Today the only way to iterate on the exploration_devtools panel is to rebuild the extension bundle (tool/build_devtools_extension.sh), restart the dogfood target, and hard-reload Chrome. That's ~30s per iteration and burns the Dart hot-reload story entirely.

`devtools_extensions` ships a simulated DevTools environment: `dart run devtools_extensions serve` (from the package dir) launches the panel as a normal Flutter web app with a fake DTD + fake VM service. Full hot reload works there.

## What this bead does

1. Add an `exploration_devtools (standalone web)` config to `.vscode/launch.json` that runs the package via the simulated environment. Wire a VS Code task so it can be debugged with the Dart extension (breakpoints in .dart files, variable inspection).
2. Document the standalone vs in-DevTools workflow in `packages/exploration_devtools/README.md` (or `docs/development.md`):
   - When to use standalone (fast iteration on widgets, layout, form behavior — anything that doesn't depend on real DTD/VM behavior)
   - When to use in-DevTools (verifying real binding handshake, real /v1/models calls, real session.run end-to-end)
   - How to attach a debugger in each mode (Dart-side via VM service URI; browser-side via Chrome source maps)
3. Cross-reference from `MANUAL_TESTS.md` so reviewers know which mode each manual smoke targets.

## Why P2

Without this, every panel iteration requires the full rebuild loop, which makes lenny-g4u-style first-frame bugs expensive to find and fix. Filing this also unblocks the work for lenny-r8f (DtdProviderConfigStore) because that has to be tested in the in-DevTools mode, not standalone.
design:
## Approach

`devtools_extensions` supports running an extension's `lib/main.dart` directly as a
Flutter **web** app against a simulated DevTools environment (fake DTD + fake VM
service) by passing `--dart-define=use_simulated_environment=true`. Run that way the
panel is a normal `flutter run -d chrome` target: Dart-Code attaches its own VM
service, so breakpoints in `exploration_devtools/lib/**` and hot reload on save both
work — none of the bundle-rebuild / Chrome-hard-reload loop. Browser-side debugging is
just Chrome DevTools on the served app (debug builds ship source maps automatically).

### Decisions

- **launch.json**: add one config, `"exploration_devtools (standalone web)"`, type
  `dart`, `request: launch`, `program: lib/main.dart`, `cwd:
  ${workspaceFolder}/packages/exploration_devtools`, `deviceId: chrome`, `args:
  ["--dart-define=use_simulated_environment=true"]`. No `preLaunchTask` — the point is
  to skip the bundle rebuild. New presentation group `4_devtools`.
- **No tasks.json change.** The `dart`/`chrome` launch config is directly debuggable;
  Dart-Code owns the VM service. (Contrast: the dogfood `sample_app` configs need the
  `Build DevTools Extension` preLaunchTask because they serve the *compiled bundle*
  inside real DevTools.)
- **Doc location**: `packages/exploration_devtools/README.md`, section "Iterating on
  the panel" — not `docs/development.md` (which does not exist).
- **Cross-reference**: edit `packages/exploration_devtools/MANUAL_TESTS.md` (the
  package-local file; there is no repo-root MANUAL_TESTS.md) to point at the README
  section and label the existing smokes as in-DevTools mode.

### Out of scope

- Wiring `DtdProviderConfigStore` (lenny-r8f) — its own bead.
- Any change to the compiled-bundle build path or the dogfood `sample_app` configs.
- `docs/manual_smoke.md` — already cross-links MANUAL_TESTS.md; no edit needed.

## Implementation Plan

1. Add the standalone-web launch config — `.vscode/launch.json`

   Insert a new object into the `configurations` array, after the
   `"exploration_cli (attach to running app)"` entry (before the closing `]`):

   ```jsonc
       {
         "name": "exploration_devtools (standalone web)",
         "type": "dart",
         "request": "launch",
         "program": "lib/main.dart",
         "cwd": "${workspaceFolder}/packages/exploration_devtools",
         "deviceId": "chrome",
         // Runs the panel as a plain Flutter web app against the
         // devtools_extensions simulated environment (fake DTD + fake VM
         // service). NO preLaunchTask: this path deliberately skips the
         // bundle rebuild (lenny-1l8) — Dart-Code attaches its own VM
         // service, so breakpoints in lib/** and hot reload on save work.
         // For the real DTD/VM handshake use the dogfood compound instead.
         "args": ["--dart-define=use_simulated_environment=true"],
         "presentation": {
           "group": "4_devtools",
           "order": 1
         }
       }
   ```

   Also add a bullet to the header `Conventions:` comment block:

   ```jsonc
   //   - "exploration_devtools (standalone web)" runs the panel via the
   //     devtools_extensions simulated environment (Chrome web target, no
   //     bundle rebuild); use it for fast widget/layout iteration. The
   //     dogfood compound is the only path that exercises real DTD/VM.
   ```

   Test:
   ```bash
   python3 -c "import json,re,sys; s=open('.vscode/launch.json').read(); s=re.sub(r'(?m)//.*$','',s); d=json.loads(s); names=[c['name'] for c in d['configurations']]; assert 'exploration_devtools (standalone web)' in names, names; c=[x for x in d['configurations'] if x['name']=='exploration_devtools (standalone web)'][0]; assert c['type']=='dart' and c['deviceId']=='chrome' and c['program']=='lib/main.dart' and c['cwd']=='\${workspaceFolder}/packages/exploration_devtools'; assert '--dart-define=use_simulated_environment=true' in c['args']; assert 'preLaunchTask' not in c; assert c['presentation']['group']=='4_devtools'; print('ok')"
   ```
   Expected: prints `ok`

   Commit:
   ```bash
   git add .vscode/launch.json
   git commit -m "feat(devtools): add standalone-web launch config for exploration_devtools panel"
   ```

2. Add the "Iterating on the panel" section — `packages/exploration_devtools/README.md`

   Insert this section immediately after the existing `## Auto-discovery` section
   (before `## Minimum versions`):

   ```markdown
   ## Iterating on the panel

   Two ways to run the panel while developing it:

   ### Standalone web (fast iteration)

   Use for widget work, layout, and form behavior — anything that does not
   depend on real DTD or real VM-service traffic. The
   `devtools_extensions` simulated environment supplies a fake DTD and a
   fake VM service, so `lib/main.dart` runs as a plain `flutter run -d
   chrome` web app: hot reload on save and source-level breakpoints both
   work, with **no** bundle rebuild (`tool/build_devtools_extension.sh`)
   in the loop.

   - VS Code: launch the **`exploration_devtools (standalone web)`** config
     (group `4_devtools` in `.vscode/launch.json`).
   - CLI equivalent: `flutter run -d chrome --dart-define=use_simulated_environment=true`
     from `packages/exploration_devtools/`.

   ### In-DevTools (real handshake)

   Use to verify the real binding handshake, real `/v1/models` calls, and
   real `session.run` end-to-end. This runs the compiled bundle inside a
   real DevTools instance attached to `sample_app`.

   - VS Code: launch the **`Dogfood: sample_app + exploration_cli`** compound
     (it runs the `Build DevTools Extension` preLaunchTask first), then open
     DevTools -> **Exploration** tab.

   ### Attaching a debugger

   - **Dart side:** launch the `exploration_devtools (standalone web)` config
     and set breakpoints in `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/**` — Dart-Code
     owns the VM service for this target, so they bind immediately.
   - **Browser side:** open Chrome DevTools on the served tab (the one
     `flutter run -d chrome` opened) — debug web builds ship source maps, so
     you can set breakpoints in the `.dart` sources from the Sources panel.
   ```

   Test:
   ```bash
   grep -q '^## Iterating on the panel' packages/exploration_devtools/README.md && grep -q 'exploration_devtools (standalone web)' packages/exploration_devtools/README.md && grep -q 'Dart side' packages/exploration_devtools/README.md && grep -q 'Browser side' packages/exploration_devtools/README.md && grep -q 'Dogfood: sample_app + exploration_cli' packages/exploration_devtools/README.md && echo ok
   ```
   Expected: prints `ok`

   Commit:
   ```bash
   git add packages/exploration_devtools/README.md
   git commit -m "docs(devtools): document standalone-web vs in-DevTools panel iteration"
   ```

3. Cross-reference the iteration modes — `packages/exploration_devtools/MANUAL_TESTS.md`

   Insert this paragraph immediately after the intro lines (after
   "(real network endpoints, real model providers)." and before the first
   `## Manual:` heading):

   ```markdown
   All smokes in this file target **in-DevTools mode** (compiled bundle in a
   real DevTools instance attached to a running app) — they exercise the real
   binding handshake and real provider calls. For fast iteration on widgets,
   layout, or form behavior that does not need real DTD/VM traffic, use
   **standalone web mode** instead; see
   [README.md -> "Iterating on the panel"](README.md#iterating-on-the-panel).
   ```

   Test:
   ```bash
   grep -q 'in-DevTools mode' packages/exploration_devtools/MANUAL_TESTS.md && grep -q 'standalone web mode' packages/exploration_devtools/MANUAL_TESTS.md && grep -q 'Iterating on the panel' packages/exploration_devtools/MANUAL_TESTS.md && echo ok
   ```
   Expected: prints `ok`

   Commit:
   ```bash
   git add packages/exploration_devtools/MANUAL_TESTS.md
   git commit -m "docs(devtools): note which iteration mode the manual smokes target"
   ```

4. Confirm the workspace still analyzes clean (no source changed, sanity gate)

   Test:
   ```bash
   dart pub get -C packages/exploration_devtools >/dev/null && dart analyze packages/exploration_devtools
   ```
   Expected: `No issues found!`

   Commit: none (verification only).

## Touches

**Files:**
- `.vscode/launch.json` — modified (new config in `configurations`; new bullet in the header comment)
- `packages/exploration_devtools/README.md` — modified (new `## Iterating on the panel` section after `## Auto-discovery`)
- `packages/exploration_devtools/MANUAL_TESTS.md` — modified (new cross-reference paragraph after the intro)

**Symbols added/exposed:** none (config + docs only; no Dart symbols).

## Validation Plan

- [ ] AC1 (launch config present + correct fields + group `4_devtools`) → `python3 -c "import json,re; s=re.sub(r'(?m)//.*$','',open('.vscode/launch.json').read()); d=json.loads(s); c=[x for x in d['configurations'] if x['name']=='exploration_devtools (standalone web)'][0]; assert c['type']=='dart' and c['deviceId']=='chrome' and c['program']=='lib/main.dart' and c['cwd']=='\${workspaceFolder}/packages/exploration_devtools' and '--dart-define=use_simulated_environment=true' in c['args'] and c['presentation']['group']=='4_devtools'; print('ok')"` → prints `ok`
- [ ] AC2 (no preLaunchTask) → `python3 -c "import json,re; s=re.sub(r'(?m)//.*$','',open('.vscode/launch.json').read()); d=json.loads(s); c=[x for x in d['configurations'] if x['name']=='exploration_devtools (standalone web)'][0]; assert 'preLaunchTask' not in c; print('ok')"` → prints `ok`
- [ ] AC3 (jsonc parses) → `python3 -c "import json,re; json.loads(re.sub(r'(?m)//.*$','',open('.vscode/launch.json').read())); print('ok')"` → prints `ok`
- [ ] AC4 (README section: standalone, in-DevTools, Dart-side repro, browser-side repro) → `grep -q '^## Iterating on the panel' packages/exploration_devtools/README.md && grep -qi 'standalone web' packages/exploration_devtools/README.md && grep -qi 'in-devtools' packages/exploration_devtools/README.md && grep -q 'Dart side' packages/exploration_devtools/README.md && grep -q 'Browser side' packages/exploration_devtools/README.md && echo ok` → prints `ok`
- [ ] AC5 (MANUAL_TESTS cross-ref + mode label) → `grep -q 'Iterating on the panel' packages/exploration_devtools/MANUAL_TESTS.md && grep -q 'in-DevTools mode' packages/exploration_devtools/MANUAL_TESTS.md && grep -q 'standalone web mode' packages/exploration_devtools/MANUAL_TESTS.md && echo ok` → prints `ok`
- [ ] AC6 (workspace analyzes clean) → `dart pub get -C packages/exploration_devtools >/dev/null && dart analyze packages/exploration_devtools` → `No issues found!`
- [ ] Manual: launch the `exploration_devtools (standalone web)` config in VS Code; Chrome opens with the panel rendered against the simulated environment; edit a widget in `lib/src/exploration_shell.dart`, save, confirm hot reload applies without a full restart; set a breakpoint in `lib/main.dart`'s `build` and confirm it binds and hits on reload.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `.vscode/launch.json` contains a configuration named `exploration_devtools (standalone web)` with `type: dart`, `request: launch`, `program: lib/main.dart`, `cwd: ${workspaceFolder}/packages/exploration_devtools`, `deviceId: chrome`, and `args` including `--dart-define=use_simulated_environment=true`, in its own presentation group `4_devtools`
- [ ] The new launch config has no `preLaunchTask` (it must not trigger the bundle rebuild)
- [ ] `.vscode/launch.json` parses as valid JSON-with-comments (jsonc) — `python3 -c` strip-comments parse succeeds
- [ ] `packages/exploration_devtools/README.md` has an "Iterating on the panel" section that (a) describes when to use standalone web mode, (b) describes when to use the in-DevTools dogfood mode, (c) gives a one-line repro for attaching a Dart-side debugger, and (d) gives a one-line repro for attaching a browser-side debugger
- [ ] `packages/exploration_devtools/MANUAL_TESTS.md` references the README "Iterating on the panel" section and states which mode (standalone vs in-DevTools) the manual smokes target
- [ ] `dart analyze packages/exploration_devtools` still passes (no source changes, but config edits must not break the workspace)

title: DevTools prompt panel: wire Start button to session.run() so the agent actually runs
description:
Closes a documented AC gap from lenny-0wd. After 0wd merged, the prompt panel builds a real `ModelProvider` from the configured provider/model and exposes it via `PromptPanelController.activeProvider`, but the controller's `start()` does not yet call `session.run()`. Pressing **Start** mints a config but doesn't actually contact the model.

## Why deferred

The 0wd spec referenced two pieces that don't exist in the agent package:

1. `TrajectoryWriter.noop()` — there's no no-op writer in `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/trajectory/writer.dart`. The panel needs one, or it needs to wire a real DTD-backed writer (DevTools panels can't write to disk).
2. `DefaultLoopHost.fromSession(...)` — exists, but expects the merged **core + plugin tool descriptors** as input. The panel currently has no way to obtain that merged map from the running app's binding.

The 0wd bitsmith chose to ship the provider+catalog plumbing and defer the actual session invocation rather than fabricate either of those.

## What this bead does

1. Add a no-op or DTD-backed writer in `exploration_agent` (or expose the panel's existing `DtdTrajectorySink` as the production target).
2. Surface a way for the panel to obtain the merged tool-descriptor map from the binding via VM service extension. Possible approach: extend the binding handshake (lenny-cx6.11) to return tool descriptors alongside the plugin manifest, OR expose a separate `ext.flutter.exploration.tools` extension.
3. Wire `PromptPanelController.start()` to call `session.run(host: DefaultLoopHost.fromSession(session, tools), provider: activeProvider, writer: ...)`. Hook `SessionStarted` / `SessionEnded` / `SessionProgressEvent` streams to drive the UI's running state (already partially wired in `prompt_tab_mount.dart`).
4. Manual AC: with a configured provider and a connected sample_app, pressing **Start** runs at least one turn end-to-end and the trajectory appears in the timeline panel.

## Touches

- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/trajectory/writer.dart` — add no-op writer (or expose an alternative)
- `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/panels/prompt_panel_controller.dart` — call session.run
- `packages/exploration_agent` (potentially) — extend binding handshake or add tools extension
- Tests covering the new wiring
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `PromptPanelController.start()` invokes `ExplorationSession.run()` with the configured `ModelProvider` and the merged `DefaultLoopHost`; calling `start()` without a `providerCfg` throws `StateError` before any session.run is attempted.
- [ ] The controller wires a real `TrajectoryWriter` backed by a `BroadcastTrajectorySink` (in-memory) for v1; a code comment on the sink field names `DtdTrajectorySink` as the future on-disk successor.
- [ ] Tool descriptors handed to `DefaultLoopHost.fromSession` come from `session.handshake.plugins` (the live binding) intersected with `PromptPanelConfig.enabledPluginNamespaces` via a shared `buildPluginTools` helper — never a hardcoded list.
- [ ] `PromptPanelController.runFuture` resolves with a `SessionTermination` when the loop exits; `PromptTabMount` observes that completion and re-enables the form (existing `SessionEnded`/`SessionStarted` handling remains; new wiring covers natural loop termination too).
- [ ] `PromptPanelController.trajectory` yields each `TrajectoryRecord` produced by the live writer; `TimelinePanelMount` is wired to consume that stream through `ExplorationShell`.
- [ ] Manual: with a configured provider and a connected `sample_app`, pressing Start runs at least one turn end-to-end and at least one `TurnRecord` is rendered in the Timeline tab. (Documented in `packages/exploration_devtools/docs/manual_smoke.md`.)

title: swift-infer: align with fs agent wire contract — Bearer auth + conversation/session/capture-bodies headers + SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN
description:
Lenny's swift-infer client diverges from the actual swift-infer wire contract that factoryskills' `fs agent` already implements correctly. Result: lenny's CLI cannot authenticate against the user's running gateway, and even if it could, none of the per-conversation tracing/inspection features the gateway provides would work.

## Reference implementation

`factoryskills/internal/agent/agent.go:734-751` — exact header set sent by `fs agent`:

```go
httpReq.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
httpReq.Header.Set("Accept", "text/event-stream")
if a.APIToken != "" {
    httpReq.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+a.APIToken)
}
if a.CaptureBodies {
    httpReq.Header.Set("X-Swift-Infer-Capture-Bodies", "true")
}
if a.ConversationID != "" {
    httpReq.Header.Set("X-Conversation-Id", a.ConversationID)
}
if a.SessionID != "" {
    httpReq.Header.Set("X-Session-Id", a.SessionID)
}
```

`factoryskills/internal/commands/agent.go:109-112`:

```go
// API token: --api-token flag, or SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN env var.
if apiToken == "" {
    apiToken = os.Getenv("SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN")
}
```

ConversationID is constructed as `fsagent-<beadID>-<unixtime>` (one per run; groups all turns).

## What lenny is doing wrong today

`packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/swift_infer/swift_infer_provider.dart:55-56`:

```dart
final key = config.apiKey;
if (key != null) h['x-api-key'] = key;   // WRONG — gateway expects Bearer
```

`packages/exploration_cli/lib/src/provider_factory.dart:30-44` — does not read any swift-infer env var, passes no key.

No support for `X-Conversation-Id`, `X-Session-Id`, or `X-Swift-Infer-Capture-Bodies` anywhere.

## What this bead does

1. **`SwiftInferConfig`** (`packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/swift_infer/swift_infer_config.dart`):
   - Rename `apiKey` → `bearerToken` (or keep `apiKey` as the Dart name but change the header to `Authorization: Bearer <value>`). Update the field doc accordingly.
   - Add `bool captureBodies = false` (when true, sends `X-Swift-Infer-Capture-Bodies: true`).
   - Add `String? conversationId` and `String? sessionId` — provider sets the headers when non-null.
   - Add `Map<String, String> extraHeaders = const {}` for forward-compat (any header `fs agent` doesn't already cover, or per-deployment custom ones). `extraHeaders` cannot overwrite the four well-known headers above (assertion / documented precedence).

2. **`SwiftInferModelProvider`** (`packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/swift_infer/swift_infer_provider.dart`):
   - Replace `x-api-key` write with `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.
   - Set `X-Swift-Infer-Capture-Bodies`, `X-Conversation-Id`, `X-Session-Id` when corresponding config fields are set.
   - Merge `extraHeaders` last (non-overwriting).

3. **CLI** (`packages/exploration_cli/lib/src/provider_factory.dart` + cli_args wiring):
   - Read `SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN` env (matches `fs agent`'s name; share the env-var contract).
   - Read `SWIFT_INFER_ENDPOINT` env (or keep the localhost:8080 default; fall through to a CLI flag if symmetry with fs agent's `--endpoint` is desired).
   - Construct one conversationId per run: `exploration-<sessionId>-<unixms>` so the swift-infer dashboard can group every turn of one exploration session.
   - Set `captureBodies: true` for dev/PoC (gives us `GET /v1/conversations/<id>` introspection for free).
   - Document both env vars + the captureBodies behavior in CLI README / AGENTS.md template, with a 'see also: fs agent' pointer.

4. **DevTools** (consumed by lenny-0wd — separate bead): the panel exposes Bearer token (masked) + endpoint + an 'extra headers' editor; it constructs `conversationId` from the active ExplorationSession id; `captureBodies` is a checkbox (default on for dev).

## Out of scope

- Server-side admin token handling (`SWIFT_INFER_ADMIN_TOKEN` is for ops curl, not the agent path).
- Streaming SSE refactor — the existing thinking() implementation already uses streaming.
- A unified config object shared between CLI and DevTools — separate frontends, separate config storages.

## Touches

- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/swift_infer/swift_infer_config.dart` — fields
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/swift_infer/swift_infer_provider.dart` — header construction
- `packages/exploration_cli/lib/src/provider_factory.dart` — env-var reads
- `packages/exploration_cli/lib/src/cli_args.dart` — optional flag for endpoint override (parity with fs agent)
- AGENTS.md template / CLI README — document env vars + reference fs agent for symmetry
- Tests on agent + CLI sides

## Why this matters

Without this fix, talking to the user's swift-infer instance from lenny is impossible (wrong auth header), and even when fixed, we'd lose the per-conversation tracing that makes the gateway debuggable — exactly the workflow `fs agent` already has working.



## Steps to Reproduce

1. Start the user's swift-infer gateway locally with auth enabled (it requires `Authorization: Bearer <SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN>`).
2. Export `SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN=sk-...` in the same shell.
3. Run the lenny CLI against the gateway: `dart run exploration_cli --model qwen-mlx --vm-uri ws://… --goal "open settings"`.
4. Observe: the request fails with `401 Unauthorized` because lenny sends `x-api-key` (which is always unset in the CLI anyway) instead of `Authorization: Bearer`.
5. Even if you bypass auth (e.g. point the gateway at an unauthenticated mode), `GET /v1/conversations/<id>` returns nothing because lenny never sets `X-Conversation-Id`, `X-Session-Id`, or `X-Swift-Infer-Capture-Bodies`.

Expected: lenny matches `fs agent`'s wire contract — Bearer auth + per-run conversation tracing + capture-bodies for inspection.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `SwiftInferConfig` exposes nullable `bearerToken`, `conversationId`, `sessionId`; `bool captureBodies` (default `false`); `Map<String, String> extraHeaders` (default `const {}`); legacy `apiKey` field is removed.
- [ ] `SwiftInferModelProvider._headers()` emits `Authorization: Bearer <token>` (not `x-api-key`) when `bearerToken != null`; omits the header entirely otherwise.
- [ ] When `captureBodies` is `true`, `_headers()` sets `X-Swift-Infer-Capture-Bodies: true`; omits otherwise.
- [ ] When `conversationId != null`, `_headers()` sets `X-Conversation-Id: <value>`; same for `sessionId` → `X-Session-Id`.
- [ ] `extraHeaders` entries are merged into the request headers but cannot overwrite `Authorization`, `X-Swift-Infer-Capture-Bodies`, `X-Conversation-Id`, or `X-Session-Id` (verified by a test that asserts well-known values win on conflict).
- [ ] `_headers()` sets `Accept: text/event-stream` (parity with `fs agent`).
- [ ] `buildProvider(ModelTier.qwenMlx)` reads `SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN` from `Platform.environment` and passes it as `bearerToken` (null/empty → unauthenticated request).
- [ ] `buildProvider(ModelTier.qwenMlx)` reads `SWIFT_INFER_ENDPOINT` and uses it as `baseUrl` when set; falls back to `http://localhost:8080` otherwise.
- [ ] `buildProvider(ModelTier.qwenMlx)` constructs `conversationId = "exploration-${sessionId}-${unixMs}"` where `sessionId` is supplied by the caller, and passes it to the config.
- [ ] `buildProvider(ModelTier.qwenMlx)` defaults `captureBodies: true`.
- [ ] `buildProvider` signature accepts a `sessionId` (and optional clock) parameter so the conversationId is deterministic in tests; all existing call sites are updated.
- [ ] `packages/exploration_cli/README.md` documents `SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN`, `SWIFT_INFER_ENDPOINT`, the `captureBodies=true` default, and references `fs agent` for the wire-contract source of truth.
- [ ] `packages/exploration_cli/templates/AGENTS.md` gains a short "## swift-infer gateway" section listing the same env vars + the `fs agent` pointer.
- [ ] Swift-infer provider tests assert: Bearer header present and equals `Bearer sk-abc`; `x-api-key` header is never present; `X-Conversation-Id`/`X-Session-Id`/`X-Swift-Infer-Capture-Bodies` set when configured; `extraHeaders` cannot overwrite the four well-known headers.
- [ ] Provider-factory tests assert: with `SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN=sk-test` set, the built `SwiftInferModelProvider` issues a request with `Authorization: Bearer sk-test`, `X-Conversation-Id` matching `exploration-<sessionId>-<unixMs>`, and `X-Swift-Infer-Capture-Bodies: true`.
- [ ] `dart analyze` and `dart test` pass for `packages/exploration_agent` and `packages/exploration_cli`.

comments.author: fs
comments.text: Complete. Branch: fs/lenny-3et/swift-infer-align-with-fs-agent-wire-con

comments.author: inspector
comments.text: inspector: APPROVED. swift-infer client now header-for-header matches fs agent: Bearer auth, X-Conversation-Id/X-Session-Id/X-Swift-Infer-Capture-Bodies, Accept: text/event-stream, extraHeaders forward-compat with well-known-wins precedence; CLI reads SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN + SWIFT_INFER_ENDPOINT, mints exploration-<sessionId>-<unixMs> conversationId, defaults captureBodies=true; README + AGENTS.md template document the contract; dart analyze clean, all swift_infer/provider_factory and full exploration_agent + exploration_cli suites pass.

title: DevTools prompt panel: wire live plugin manifest from binding handshake
description:
`packages/exploration_devtools/lib/main.dart` constructs `ExplorationShell` without passing `plugins`, so the shell defaults to `plugins: const []`. The prompt panel renders the empty-state hint instead of real toggles, even when the connected app loaded plugins.

The handshake protocol that exposes the manifest already exists (cx6.11 — `ext.flutter.exploration.handshake` returns active-plugin manifest). The panel just doesn't call it.

## Architecture

Resolve the plugin manifest at panel-mount time via the same VM service URI / DTD path the timeline panel uses. Keep it reactive — if the user reconnects to a different app, the manifest re-resolves.

Plug the result into the existing `ExplorationShell.plugins` parameter so `PromptPanel` renders the real per-namespace toggles (router/riverpod/dio/etc.).

## Touches

- `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/main.dart` — request the manifest before/after `DevToolsExtension` mounts; pipe into the shell
- `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/exploration_shell.dart` — accept a `Stream<List<PluginManifestEntry>>` or use a controller; rebuild on change
- `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/panel_host.dart` — possibly host the manifest controller alongside VmServiceUriResolver
- `packages/exploration_agent` — verify `ExplorationSession` exposes the handshake-result manifest (it does for cx6.11; confirm the public API)
- `packages/exploration_devtools/test/...` — fake handshake response → plugins render; empty manifest → empty-state hint

## Acceptance
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] On panel mount with a connected VM service URI, `ExplorationPanelHost` calls the binding handshake via `VmServiceClient.handshake()` exactly once and exposes the result through a `ValueListenable<ManifestProbeResult>`.
- [ ] When the probe returns a non-empty plugin list, `PromptPanel` renders one `CheckboxListTile` per `PluginManifestEntry.namespace` (key `prompt.plugin.<namespace>`) and no `prompt.pluginsEmpty` Text widget.
- [ ] When the probe returns an empty plugin list, `PromptPanel` renders the existing `prompt.pluginsEmpty` hint and zero `prompt.plugin.*` toggles.
- [ ] When the probe throws `BindingNotInitializedError`, the Prompt tab renders a banner with key `prompt.bindingNotDetected` containing the text "Binding not detected" and does NOT render the empty-plugin guide hint.
- [ ] When the probe is in flight, the Prompt tab renders a `CircularProgressIndicator` with key `prompt.manifestLoading` and no toggles or banners.
- [ ] When the production listenable wired to `serviceManager.connectedState` notifies (reconnect), `ExplorationPanelHost.refreshManifest()` re-runs the probe and the `ValueListenable<ManifestProbeResult>` emits the new value; subscribed widgets rebuild.
- [ ] Calling `refreshManifest()` while a previous probe is in flight cancels the previous result (only the latest probe's outcome is published).
- [ ] `flutter test packages/exploration_devtools/test/manifest_probe_test.dart` and the updated `shell_test.dart` and `panels/prompt_panel_test.dart` pass.
- [ ] `dart analyze packages/exploration_devtools` reports zero issues.
- [ ] `flutter run -t packages/exploration_flutter/example/sample_app/lib/main.dart -d chrome` with the DevTools extension attached shows three plugin toggles (`router`, `riverpod`, `dio`) populated in the Prompt tab without entering the empty-state hint.

AC10 (sample_app dogfood with 'flutter run -d chrome') requires an interactive browser session and is not automatable from the bitsmith worktree; the behavior it gates is covered by AC2 (toggles render from probe), AC3 (empty list -> empty-state), and AC6 (reactive re-probe via vmServiceUriListenable).

title: DevTools prompt panel: provider config + dynamic model list from /v1/models
description:
Make the DevTools extension actually usable end-to-end by replacing the placeholder `_defaultAvailableModels` (one fake 'default' row) with a real provider configuration layer + a model dropdown populated from each provider's `/v1/models` API.

## Context

`packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/exploration_shell.dart:18-20` ships a single `ModelDescriptor(id: 'default', label: 'Default')` placeholder — a TODO left over from cx6.14/.15/.16. The CLI has the real provider registry (`exploration_cli/lib/src/provider_factory.dart` → qwenMlx/claude/openai) but the panel never sees it. Today you cannot pick which model to run from DevTools, so the prompt panel is decorative.

## Why now

PoC has shipped — the next step is a human dogfooding the agent end-to-end from the panel. That requires picking a model from the panel.

## Per-provider auth shape (verified against existing code + the fs agent reference impl)

- **swift-infer**: lenny-3et fixes the wire contract (Bearer auth, X-Conversation-Id, X-Session-Id, X-Swift-Infer-Capture-Bodies). The DevTools panel surfaces:
  - **Bearer token** (masked input, required for non-localhost gateways) — submitted as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.
  - **Endpoint** (default `http://localhost:8080`).
  - **Capture bodies** (checkbox, default ON for dev — sends `X-Swift-Infer-Capture-Bodies: true` so the gateway persists request/response for `GET /v1/conversations/<id>` introspection).
  - **conversationId** is computed automatically from the active `ExplorationSession` id (`exploration-<sessionId>-<unixms>`); displayed read-only as a debug breadcrumb the user can copy and paste into a `curl` against the swift-infer admin API.
  - **Extra headers editor**: small key/value list; merged into every request after the four well-known ones (cannot overwrite them).
  - Symmetry with `fs agent`: documented in the panel that this is the same wire contract `fs agent` uses, plus a one-line "see also" pointer.
- **Anthropic**: `apiKey: String` required (CLI reads `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` env, see `provider_factory.dart:47`). Panel is a webapp inside Chrome — no `Platform.environment` access. **Panel MUST prompt for the key** (masked) + optional base URL override.
- **OpenAI**: identical to Anthropic, env var `OPENAI_API_KEY`. Same panel inputs.

## Architecture

Two layers, both new in `exploration_devtools`:

1. **Provider configuration** — small per-provider config form. Persist via DTD (preferred — survives reloads, scoped to the workspace) or `window.localStorage` (fallback). Keys/tokens redacted in the UI; never logged.
2. **Dynamic model list** — when a provider is selected (and configured), fetch `GET /v1/models` from that provider's base URL.
   - Anthropic + OpenAI: include the configured key.
   - swift-infer: send `Authorization: Bearer <token>` + the four well-known headers (the gateway probably enforces auth on /v1/models too).
   - Merge against the local `ModelCapabilities` table:
     - Models with known capability metadata → labeled with vision/thinking badges
     - Models without → still listed, with a '⚠ unknown capabilities' marker; conservative defaults (vision=false, preserve_thinking=false)
   - Cache result in memory for the session; refetch on explicit reload-button click

## Wire format

- Anthropic: `GET /v1/models` → `{data: [{id, display_name, ...}]}`
- OpenAI: `GET /v1/models` → `{object: 'list', data: [{id, owned_by, ...}]}`
- swift-infer: speaks Anthropic-compat by default; expect Anthropic shape. Verify against actual swift-infer response in implementation — if it does not implement `/v1/models`, fall back to a small static list seeded from `SwiftInferConfig`'s known model id and surface a 'using fallback list' badge.

## Out of scope (file separately if needed)

- Per-model parameter overrides UI (temp/top_p sliders) — capability defaults are fine for v1.
- Provider connection diagnostics dashboard — a single 'Test connection' button is enough.
- Sharing config with the CLI's env-based reader — DevTools config is panel-local.

## Touches

- `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/panels/provider_config.dart` — new (per-provider form)
- `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/panels/prompt_panel.dart` — consumes resolved `AvailableModels` stream/state instead of static prop
- `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/exploration_shell.dart` — wire provider config + model-list controller
- `packages/exploration_agent` — expose `ModelCapabilities` lookup as a public surface (`capabilitiesFor(providerId, modelId)`)
- `packages/exploration_devtools/test/...` — fake HTTP client tests for /v1/models per provider, masked-input widget test, capability-merge test, swift-infer Bearer-header assertion, captureBodies + conversationId headers test

## Depends on

- **lenny-3et** — swift-infer wire contract fix in the agent package; this bead consumes the corrected `SwiftInferConfig` API surface.
design:
## Implementation Plan

1. **Capabilities lookup — `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/capabilities_lookup.dart`** (new). Top-level `ModelCapabilities? capabilitiesFor(String providerId, String modelId)` switching on `providerId`: `'anthropic'` → returns vision-tier caps `(vision:true, preserveThinking:false, maxContext:200000, supportsToolUse:true)` iff `kAnthropicVisionModels.contains(modelId)` else null; `'openai'` → `openAiModels[modelId]?.capabilities`; `'swift-infer'` → returns qwen-tier caps `(vision:true, preserveThinking:true, maxContext:32768, supportsToolUse:true)` iff `modelId.startsWith('qwen3')` else null; default → null. Promote `_visionModels` in `anthropic_provider.dart` to public `const kAnthropicVisionModels`; export the new lookup from `provider/provider.dart`.
   Test: `dart test packages/exploration_agent/test/provider/capabilities_lookup_test.dart` → PASS (known hit, unknown miss, swift-infer prefix match).
   Commit: `feat(exploration_agent): expose capabilitiesFor lookup`

2. **ProviderConfig value types — `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/panels/provider_config.dart`** (new). Sealed `ProviderConfig` with required `id` (`'swift-infer'|'anthropic'|'openai'`), `defaultModelId`, `Uri get baseUrl`, `Map<String,String> headersFor(String conversationId)`, `Map<String,dynamic> toJson()` (unredacted, persistence only), `Map<String,dynamic> toJsonRedacted()` (for logs/display). Variants: `SwiftInferUiConfig` (bearerToken, endpoint, captureBodies, extraHeaders), `AnthropicUiConfig` (apiKey, baseUrlOverride), `OpenAiUiConfig` (apiKey, baseUrlOverride). Secrets live in private fields and never appear in `toString` / `toJsonRedacted`. Header-merge precedence (swift-infer): `extraHeaders` first, then well-known headers (Authorization, X-Conversation-Id, X-Session-Id, X-Swift-Infer-Capture-Bodies, Content-Type, Accept) overwrite — assert in test.
   Test: `flutter test packages/exploration_devtools/test/panels/provider_config_test.dart` → PASS.
   Commit: `feat(devtools): provider config value types`

3. **Persistence — `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/panels/provider_config_store.dart`** (new). Abstract `ProviderConfigStore` + `InMemoryProviderConfigStore` + `DtdProviderConfigStore` keyed by `lenny.providerConfig.<id>` via `DartToolingDaemon.{set,get}IDEWorkspaceProperty`. Stores the FULL token (workspace properties are local-only); SECURITY comment block flags the tradeoff.
   Test: `flutter test packages/exploration_devtools/test/panels/provider_config_store_test.dart` → PASS — round-trip with fake DTD; missing key returns null.
   Commit: `feat(devtools): DTD-backed provider config store`

4. **ModelCatalog — `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/panels/model_catalog.dart`** (new). `ModelCatalog({http.Client? client})` with `Future<List<ResolvedModel>> fetch(ProviderConfig cfg, {bool reload = false})`. Per-provider request/parse:
   - anthropic: `GET <base>/v1/models`, parse `{data:[{id, display_name}]}`.
   - openai: `GET <base>/v1/models`, parse `{data:[{id}]}`.
   - swift-infer: same path + four well-known headers + extraHeaders.
   `reload: true` bypasses an in-memory cache. 401/network errors throw `ModelCatalogError(statusCode)` for anthropic+openai (UI banner); swift-infer 404/refused/parse error falls back to `[ResolvedModel(cfg.defaultModelId, usingFallback: true)]` because the gateway may not implement `/v1/models`. Capability merge happens here via `capabilitiesFor(cfg.id, modelId)`; null → `ResolvedModel.capabilities = null` → unknown badge. Types: `ResolvedModel { String id, label; ModelCapabilities? capabilities; bool usingFallback; }`, `ModelCatalogError implements Exception { int? statusCode; }`.
   Test: `flutter test packages/exploration_devtools/test/panels/model_catalog_test.dart` → PASS — header assertions per provider, parse, cache hit, swift-infer fallback, anthropic 401 surfaces error, capability merge.
   Commit: `feat(devtools): model catalog with /v1/models + fallback + capability merge`

5. **`ProviderConfigForm` widget in `provider_config.dart`** (same file, below the value types). `DropdownButtonFormField<String>` (`Key('providerForm.providerSelect')`) for provider id plus `AnimatedSwitcher` between three subforms keyed `Key('providerForm.<id>')`. swift-infer subform fields: bearer token (`Key('providerForm.swift-infer.bearer')`, `obscureText: true`), endpoint, captureBodies `Switch`, extraHeaders editor (`ListView` of `Row(TextField,TextField,IconButton(remove))` + `Add header` button), conversationId breadcrumb (`SelectableText`, read-only, `Key('providerForm.swift-infer.conversationId')`). anthropic/openai subforms: api key (`obscureText: true`), optional base URL, default model id. `Test connection` button calls `ModelCatalog.fetch(cfg, reload: true)` and renders inline success/error. `onChanged: (ProviderConfig)` upward; parent persists via `ProviderConfigStore.save`.
   Test: `flutter test packages/exploration_devtools/test/panels/provider_config_form_test.dart` → PASS — bearer + apiKey both `obscureText: true`; captureBodies toggle; extraHeaders add/remove; conversationId selectable but not editable.
   Commit: `feat(devtools): provider config form widget`

6. **Wire into prompt panel + shell — `prompt_panel.dart`, `prompt_tab_mount.dart`, `exploration_shell.dart`**. Replace `_defaultAvailableModels` and the `availableModels` parameter chain with a `ModelCatalogState`: `({ProviderConfig? config, List<ResolvedModel> models, bool loading, Object? error})`. Shell owns one `ProviderConfigStore` + one `ModelCatalog`; `PromptTabMount` listens to a `ValueListenable<ModelCatalogState>` and triggers refresh on config changes. Above the existing model dropdown render `ProviderConfigForm` + reload button (`Key('prompt.modelsReload')`). Each `DropdownMenuItem` shows the model id plus capability badges (`vision`, `thinking`, `⚠ unknown`, `using fallback`) per `ResolvedModel.capabilities` / `usingFallback`. Error state renders `Key('prompt.modelsError')` banner without disabling the rest of the form.
   Test: `flutter test packages/exploration_devtools/test/panels/prompt_panel_test.dart` → PASS — existing tests still green; new test pumps with a fake catalog and asserts badges render and reload button refetches.
   Commit: `feat(devtools): prompt panel consumes ModelCatalog state`

7. **Provider construction — new `panel_provider_factory.dart` + extend `prompt_panel_controller.dart`**. Top-level `ModelProvider buildPanelProvider(ProviderConfig cfg, String modelId, String sessionId, {DateTime Function() now = DateTime.now})` switches on the sealed config: `SwiftInferUiConfig` → `SwiftInferModelProvider` whose `SwiftInferConfig` carries `baseUrl`, `model: modelId`, `bearerToken`, `captureBodies`, `conversationId: 'exploration-$sessionId-${now().millisecondsSinceEpoch}'`, `sessionId`, `extraHeaders`, `enableVision: capabilitiesFor('swift-infer', modelId)?.vision ?? false`; `AnthropicUiConfig` → `AnthropicModelProvider(model: modelId, apiKey, endpoint: baseUrlOverride)`; `OpenAiUiConfig` → `OpenAiModelProvider(modelId, apiKey, endpoint: baseUrlOverride)`. `PromptPanelController` gains a `ModelProvider Function(ProviderConfig, String, String)` factory parameter (default `buildPanelProvider`). On `start(cfg)`, after `session.start`, build the provider from the active `ProviderConfig` + selected `modelId` and fire-and-forget `session.run(host: DefaultLoopHost.fromSession(...), provider: provider, writer: TrajectoryWriter.noop())`; completion drives `SessionEnded`. Unknown-capability models default to `enableVision: false`, `preserveThinking: false`.
   Test: `flutter test packages/exploration_devtools/test/panels/prompt_panel_controller_test.dart` → PASS — fake factory observes the selected `ProviderConfig` + `modelId`; swift-infer arm asserts the constructed `SwiftInferConfig` carries `Authorization: Bearer` and `X-Conversation-Id`.
   Commit: `feat(devtools): build provider from panel config and run session`

8. **Redaction guard — `provider_config_redaction_test.dart`**. For each variant: instantiate with a sentinel secret; assert `expect(cfg.toString(), isNot(contains(secret)))` and `expect(jsonEncode(cfg.toJsonRedacted()), isNot(contains(secret)))`. Grep panel package: no `print(`/`debugPrint(` references token/key fields.
   Test: `flutter test packages/exploration_devtools/test/panels/provider_config_redaction_test.dart` → PASS.
   Commit: `test(devtools): assert tokens never logged`

9. **Manual smoke checklist — `packages/exploration_devtools/MANUAL_TESTS.md`** (append). title: DevTools extension: build script + commit policy + auto-rebuild on lib/ changes
description:
The DevTools extension scaffolded by lenny-cx6.21 ships `extension/devtools/config.yaml` but never produces the compiled web bundle DevTools loads. When the extension changes (or on any working tree without a prior local build), opening the Exploration tab in DevTools renders only `could not read file as String: devtools_extensions/exploration_0.0.1/index.html`. Add a single shell script that builds and copies the bundle into both `exploration_devtools` (for standalone dev) and `exploration_flutter` (the host package whose pubspec dep triggers auto-discovery in consumer apps), document it in both READMEs, and run it from CI so drift can't silently land.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Fresh clone of the repo (no prior local build artifacts).
2. `cd packages/exploration_flutter/example/sample_app && flutter pub get && flutter run -d macos`.
3. Open DevTools for the running app and click the **Exploration** tab.

**Expected:** the Exploration panel renders.

**Actual:** the tab shows the error string `could not read file as String: devtools_extensions/exploration_0.0.1/index.html`, because `packages/exploration_flutter/extension/devtools/build/` is empty (gitignored, never produced).

The same failure mode reappears any time `packages/exploration_devtools/{lib,web,pubspec.yaml}` changes without a manual rebuild — there is no script, no documentation, and no CI gate that catches it.
design:
## Approach

Add `tool/build_devtools_extension.sh` at the repo root. It runs the existing `dart run devtools_extensions build_and_copy` helper twice — once into each destination — so a single invocation produces a coherent pair of bundles. Document the script in both relevant READMEs. Wire CI to run the script before the analyze/test job so any change to the extension's source triggers a fresh build automatically and PRs that break the bundle fail at merge time.

## Decisions locked in during discovery

- **Rebuild on demand; never commit.** `.gitignore` blanket `build/` rule (line 21) stays. No `!`-exceptions. ~74MB of compiled output never enters git history. Every developer and every CI run pays a ~16–30s rebuild on a fresh checkout. The artifact is mechanically reproducible, drift is the failure mode we care about, and the workspace is not currently published to pub.
- **CI just rebuilds; no checksum / verify step.** Because we don't commit the bundle, there's nothing to diff against. CI runs `tool/build_devtools_extension.sh` ahead of analyze/test; if the build fails, CI fails — that is the drift gate.
- **Plain shell script at `tool/build_devtools_extension.sh`.** Plain bash, executable, two `dart run` invocations. Discoverable via `tool/`, idiomatic in Flutter monorepos, no new tooling. Cross-platform concern is moot — the surrounding development workflow (`flutter run -d macos`, sample_app) is macOS-only for v1, and CI runs Ubuntu where bash is available.

## Implementation Plan

1. Create `tool/build_devtools_extension.sh` (new, executable).
   ```bash
   #!/usr/bin/env bash
   # tool/build_devtools_extension.sh
   #
   # Builds the exploration_devtools panel and copies the compiled web
   # bundle into both extension/devtools/build/ destinations:
   #
   #   - packages/exploration_devtools/extension/devtools/build/
   #       Used for standalone development against devtools_extensions's
   #       simulated DevTools env.
   #
   #   - packages/exploration_flutter/extension/devtools/build/
   #       The host package whose pubspec dep triggers DevTools'
   #       auto-discovery in consumer apps (sample_app et al.).
   #
   # Both destinations are gitignored — re-run this script after any
   # change to packages/exploration_devtools/{lib,web,pubspec.yaml}.
   # CI runs this script before analyze/test so PRs that break the build
   # fail at merge.
   set -euo pipefail
   ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
   cd "$ROOT/packages/exploration_devtools"
   dart run devtools_extensions build_and_copy \
     --source=. \
     --dest=extension/devtools
   dart run devtools_extensions build_and_copy \
     --source=. \
     --dest=../exploration_flutter/extension/devtools
   echo "✓ exploration_devtools bundles built into both destinations"
   ```
   Then `chmod +x tool/build_devtools_extension.sh`.
   Test: `rm -rf packages/exploration_devtools/extension/devtools/build packages/exploration_flutter/extension/devtools/build && ./tool/build_devtools_extension.sh && test -f packages/exploration_devtools/extension/devtools/build/index.html && test -f packages/exploration_flutter/extension/devtools/build/index.html && echo OK` → expect `✓ exploration_devtools bundles built into both destinations` followed by `OK`.
   Commit: `feat(tool): add build_devtools_extension.sh for dual-destination DevTools bundle build`

2. Update `packages/exploration_devtools/README.md` — insert a Build section above the existing "Auto-discovery" section.
   ```markdown
   ## Build

   The compiled web bundle that DevTools loads is **not committed**. Every
   fresh clone (and every rebuild after a change to this package's `lib/`,
   `web/`, or `pubspec.yaml`) must run:

   ```sh
   ./tool/build_devtools_extension.sh
   ```

   The script runs `dart run devtools_extensions build_and_copy` twice
   and populates both `packages/exploration_devtools/extension/devtools/build/`
   (used for standalone development) and
   `packages/exploration_flutter/extension/devtools/build/` (the host
   package whose pubspec dep triggers DevTools auto-discovery in consumer
   apps such as `sample_app`).

   CI runs the same script before analyze/test, so a PR that breaks the
   extension build fails at merge time — there is no committed-bundle
   drift to diff against.
   ```
   Test: `grep -q 'tool/build_devtools_extension.sh' packages/exploration_devtools/README.md && grep -q 'pubspec.yaml' packages/exploration_devtools/README.md` → expect exit 0.
   Commit: `docs(exploration_devtools): document build script in README`

3. Update `packages/exploration_flutter/README.md` — add a one-line reference to the build script. Append a short note at the bottom of the existing README pointing to the script and the host-package role.
   ```markdown
   ## DevTools panel

   This package ships `extension/devtools/config.yaml` so any app that
   transitively depends on `exploration_flutter` automatically surfaces
   the **Exploration** tab in DevTools. The compiled web bundle lives at
   `extension/devtools/build/` and is **not committed** — rebuild it from
   the repo root with:

   ```sh
   ./tool/build_devtools_extension.sh
   ```

   See `packages/exploration_devtools/README.md` for the full build
   workflow.
   ```
   Test: `grep -q 'tool/build_devtools_extension.sh' packages/exploration_flutter/README.md` → expect exit 0.
   Commit: `docs(exploration_flutter): reference DevTools build script in README`

4. Wire CI in `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` — insert one `run` step after the existing `dart pub get` step and before the `flutter test packages/exploration_devtools --exclude-tags=perf` step. Place it before `dart analyze` for fail-fast on broken extension source.
   ```yaml
         - run: dart pub get
         - run: ./tool/build_devtools_extension.sh
         - run: dart analyze
   ```
   Test: `grep -n 'build_devtools_extension.sh' .github/workflows/ci.yaml` → expect a single matching line that sits between `dart pub get` and `dart analyze`.
   Commit: `ci: build DevTools extension bundle before analyze/test`

5. Verify `.gitignore` is unchanged at line 21 and no exception was added.
   Test: `sed -n '21p' .gitignore` → expect `build/` and `! grep -q 'extension/devtools/build' .gitignore` → expect exit 0.
   Commit: (none — no change).

## Touches

**Files:**
- `tool/build_devtools_extension.sh` — new, executable, two `dart run devtools_extensions build_and_copy` invocations.
- `packages/exploration_devtools/README.md` — modified, adds a Build section above Auto-discovery.
- `packages/exploration_flutter/README.md` — modified, appends a DevTools panel section referencing the script.
- `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` — modified, adds one `- run: ./tool/build_devtools_extension.sh` step between `dart pub get` and `dart analyze`.

**Symbols added/exposed:**
- None. No Dart/library symbols change. The only new artifact is a shell entrypoint discoverable as `tool/build_devtools_extension.sh`.

## Validation Plan

- [ ] Single-command dual-destination build → `rm -rf packages/exploration_devtools/extension/devtools/build packages/exploration_flutter/extension/devtools/build && ./tool/build_devtools_extension.sh && test -f packages/exploration_devtools/extension/devtools/build/index.html && test -f packages/exploration_flutter/extension/devtools/build/index.html` → expect both `test` invocations to exit 0 and the script to print `✓ exploration_devtools bundles built into both destinations`.
- [ ] Script is executable → `test -x tool/build_devtools_extension.sh` → expect exit 0.
- [ ] exploration_devtools README documents the script and rebuild trigger → `grep -q 'tool/build_devtools_extension.sh' packages/exploration_devtools/README.md && grep -q 'pubspec.yaml' packages/exploration_devtools/README.md` → expect exit 0.
- [ ] exploration_flutter README references the script in the auto-discovery section → `grep -q 'tool/build_devtools_extension.sh' packages/exploration_flutter/README.md` → expect exit 0.
- [ ] CI runs the script after `dart pub get` and before `dart analyze` → `grep -n -E '(dart pub get|build_devtools_extension|dart analyze)' .github/workflows/ci.yaml` → expect three lines in that order.
- [ ] `.gitignore` policy is unchanged → `sed -n '21p' .gitignore` outputs `build/` and `grep -q 'extension/devtools/build' .gitignore` exits non-zero (no exception added).
- [ ] CI fails when the extension build is broken → push a branch that introduces a syntax error in `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/main.dart`; the `./tool/build_devtools_extension.sh` step in `ci.yaml` exits non-zero and `dart analyze` / test steps never run. Revert before merge.
- [ ] sample_app DevTools panel renders on a fresh clone → from a fresh checkout, run `./tool/build_devtools_extension.sh`, then `cd packages/exploration_flutter/example/sample_app && flutter run -d macos`, open DevTools, switch to the Exploration tab → expect the panel to render without the `could not read file as String: devtools_extensions/exploration_0.0.1/index.html` error.

## What was ruled out and why

- **Commit the bundles (with `.gitignore` exceptions)**. Rejected: 74MB of binary churn per change, plus drift becomes a silent failure mode.
- **Tag-time precompute / release artifact**. Rejected as scope creep; we don't have a release process yet.
- **Pure-Dart `tool/` package**. Rejected: extra setup with no portability win for a macOS/Ubuntu-only v1 workflow.
- **Makefile target**. Rejected: introduces a new toolchain (make) for one script.
- **CI verify-bundle-matches-committed**. Moot — we don't commit.

## Out of scope

- Changing the existing `.gitignore` `build/` rule.
- Publishing to pub or any release-time pre-build hook.
- Any DevTools extension panel content changes.
- Touching the existing extension source under `packages/exploration_devtools/{lib,web}` (except as a transient test in step (CI fails when broken) which is reverted).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `tool/build_devtools_extension.sh` exists, is executable (`test -x`), and from a clean checkout (`build/` dirs deleted) one invocation produces both `packages/exploration_devtools/extension/devtools/build/index.html` and `packages/exploration_flutter/extension/devtools/build/index.html`
- [ ] `packages/exploration_devtools/README.md` contains a Build section that names `tool/build_devtools_extension.sh` and states it must be re-run after changes to `lib/`, `web/`, or `pubspec.yaml`
- [ ] `packages/exploration_flutter/README.md` references `tool/build_devtools_extension.sh` in the section that mentions DevTools auto-discovery
- [ ] `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` runs `./tool/build_devtools_extension.sh` after `dart pub get` and before the `flutter test packages/exploration_devtools` step, so a broken extension build fails CI before tests run
- [ ] `.gitignore` line 21 (`build/`) is unchanged and no `!`-exception for `extension/devtools/build/` is added — bundles stay out of git
- [ ] After running the script, `flutter run -d macos` from `packages/exploration_flutter/example/sample_app/` opens the DevTools Exploration tab and renders the panel without the `could not read file as String: devtools_extensions/exploration_0.0.1/index.html` error
notes: Filed while debugging sample_app's DevTools tab after lenny-3wt landed. Local build artifacts are present in this checkout but gitignored; rebuild them via the build_and_copy invocations recorded above if they go stale.
close_reason: DevTools extension build script + CI wiring merged via PR #34. tool/build_devtools_extension.sh produces both bundles in one shot; CI rebuilds on every PR; .gitignore policy unchanged.

title: ExplorationBinding install ordering — compose with apps that bring up WidgetsFlutterBinding before us
description: The current `ExplorationBinding.ensureInitialized(plugins: [...])` API requires the user's `main()` to construct shared instances (`ProviderContainer`, `Dio`, `GoRouter`) before passing them to the binding — but constructing those very objects (notably `GoRouter` 14.x) calls `WidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized()` internally, claiming the binding slot before our type can. The PRD §6.5 install gate then throws `StateError: another WidgetsBinding (WidgetsFlutterBinding) is already active`, which is exactly what blocks `sample_app` from booting. Add a Flutter-shaped high-level entry point — `ExplorationBinding.run(ExplorationHost host)` — that claims the binding slot first and runs the user's wiring code (plugin construction, runApp) inside a callback. The existing `ensureInitialized(plugins:)` stays for tests and advanced use; only app code is steered to the new API.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `ExplorationBinding.run(ExplorationApp app)` is exported from `package:exploration_flutter/exploration_flutter.dart`.
- [ ] Calling `ExplorationBinding.run(app)` as the first Flutter-touching line of `main()` installs `ExplorationBinding` as the active `WidgetsBinding` (i.e. `WidgetsBinding.instance is ExplorationBinding` is true) before `app.build(ctx)` is invoked.
- [ ] `app.build(ctx)` receives an `ExplorationAppContext` exposing `binding`, `isProductionMode` (true iff `!kDebugMode && !kProfileMode`), and `onTeardown(Future<void> Function() cb)`.
- [ ] In debug/profile, `run` registers the plugins returned by `ExplorationAppConfig.plugins` through the same code path that `ensureInitialized(plugins:)` uses today (CorePlugin first, user plugins in order, namespace de-dup logged, lifecycle initialization scheduled via microtask).
- [ ] In debug/profile, `run` calls `runApp(config.app)` inside the zone returned by `ExplorationBinding.stabilityZoneSpec(binding)` so user microtasks flip the binding's `pendingMicrotasks` edge signal.
- [ ] In release (`kReleaseMode`), `run` skips binding installation and plugin registration, still constructs an `ExplorationAppContext` with `isProductionMode == true`, still invokes `app.build(ctx)`, and still calls `runApp(config.app)`.
- [ ] If `WidgetsBinding._instance` is already set to a non-`ExplorationBinding` type when `run` is called (e.g. a prior `WidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized()` call), `run` throws `StateError` whose message names `ExplorationBinding.run` as the recommended entry point and tells the user to make it the first line of `main()`.
- [ ] Calling `ExplorationBinding.run(app)` twice in the same process is safe: the second call is an idempotent no-op for binding installation and plugin registration, but `app.build(ctx)` and `runApp(config.app)` still run.
- [ ] Teardown callbacks registered via `ctx.onTeardown(cb)` execute in LIFO order when `ExplorationBinding.debugReset()` is called (debug/profile) and complete before `debugReset` returns control.
- [ ] `ExplorationBinding.ensureInitialized(plugins:)` keeps its existing public signature, return type, and semantics — all 14+ existing tests under `packages/exploration_flutter/test/` pass without modification.
- [ ] `examples/sample_app/lib/main.dart` is migrated to `ExplorationBinding.run(SampleAppHost())` and boots end-to-end on macOS via `flutter run -d macos` to the Login screen without throwing.
- [ ] PRD §6.1 and §6.5 are amended to document `run(app)` as the recommended entry point and `ensureInitialized(plugins:)` as the advanced/test surface.
close_reason: ExplorationBinding.run(ExplorationApp) entry point merged via PR #33. Sample_app boots cleanly; install-ordering bug fixed. PRD §6.1/§6.5 amended. ensureInitialized(plugins:) preserved unchanged for tests/advanced use.

description:
After the PoC ships with swift-infer (.15) as its local provider, add Anthropic (.36) and OpenAI (.37) to the CLI's provider map for full v1 §16 coverage.

Scope:
- Add 'anthropic' and 'openai' to the --provider CLI flag's accepted values
- Document Anthropic and OpenAI config (API keys via env, base URL override) in AGENTS.md template
- Smoke test: CLI with --provider=anthropic and --provider=openai each completes one turn end-to-end against a real API endpoint

title: swift-infer model provider (custom inference server, OpenAI + Anthropic compat)
description:
User's custom Swift-based inference server, used to debug and measure agent harness quality. Speaks both OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions and Anthropic-compatible /v1/messages, and emits debug/measurement signals beyond the standard surface (TBD what exactly — review repo source). Slots in as a sibling to .15 (MLX), .36 (Anthropic), .37 (OpenAI) under the .14 ModelProvider abstraction.

DISCOVERY GATE: do not specify until reviewing:
- ../swift-infer source (wire shape, custom fields, capability surface)
- ../factoryskills 'fs agent' implementation (how Anthropic uses it as a reference client; what hooks/measurements it surfaces)

The decision the spec needs to land:
1. Reuse .36/.37 helpers (FrontierDefaults, VisionImage, validateToolArgs, lookupTool) via OpenAI- and Anthropic-compat paths, OR build a thin third path that pulls those helpers AND adds the measurement extensions.
2. What custom fields the server emits and how they surface — e.g. into ThinkingDelta, ModelDecision metadata, or trajectory-level instrumentation records (.19 schema).
3. Whether the user's server has a single endpoint that auto-detects the compat shape, or two distinct endpoints. Affects provider config.

PRD context: swift-infer is not named in v0.5 PRD §16. Filed as a v1 add-on rather than a critical-path dep — does not block .20 (CLI). The user can promote it to ship-in-v1 if desired; current default is 'available before v1 acceptance, ships when ready.'

Cross-refs: PRD §16.1 (provider abstraction), §16.4 (other model tiers — frames swift-infer as another tier).
close_reason: Merged into .15 — swift-infer is the MLX gateway, not a separate tier

title: OpenAI frontier model provider
description: ModelProvider implementation for GPT-5 class via OpenAI's HTTP API. Reuses the frontier-tier helpers (FrontierDefaults, VisionImage, validateToolArgs, lookupTool) introduced by the Anthropic provider. Different response shape: tool_calls / function.arguments instead of tool_use blocks. Vision support per model capability metadata. Web-compatible (HTTP only, no dart:io).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `OpenAiModelProvider` in `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/openai/openai_provider.dart` implements `ModelProvider` against the OpenAI Chat Completions tools[] API; `dart analyze packages/exploration_agent` passes
- [ ] `decide(PromptPayload, ActionSchema)` parses `choices[0].message.tool_calls[]` (with `function.name` + `function.arguments` JSON string) into a `ModelDecision` per .14's contract, including `summary_update` / `rationale` / `wait_strategy` extracted from a sibling text content part when present
- [ ] When the prompt's user messages include a screenshot byte payload, an `image_url` content part with `data:image/png;base64,...` is attached to the request body (verified by mock-client request capture)
- [ ] On `SchemaRejection` from `ActionSchema.validate`, the provider re-issues the request exactly once with the validation error injected as a system note; a second rejection propagates the `SchemaRejection`
- [ ] Frontier tier defaults from `FrontierDefaults` (sourced from .36) are applied to every request: `temperature`, `topP`, `maxOutputTokens`, retry budget — verified by request-body inspection in tests
- [ ] `capabilities` returns `ModelCapabilities(vision: <per-model>, preserveThinking: false, supportsToolUse: true, maxContext: <per-model>)` for at least `gpt-5` and `gpt-5-mini` model IDs
- [ ] `thinking()` returns a `Stream<ThinkingDelta>` driven by the `/chat/completions` streaming endpoint (`stream: true`); each `delta.content` chunk emits a `ThinkingDelta(text, isFinal: false)` and the terminating chunk emits `isFinal: true`
- [ ] Mock-client tests cover: (a) happy-path tool_calls parsing, (b) vision payload attached when screenshot present, (c) schema-rejection retry-once then propagate, (d) frontier defaults present in request body, (e) streaming deltas surface as `ThinkingDelta`s
- [ ] Package remains web-compatible: a `@TestOn('vm')` web-compat test asserts no `dart:io` import in `lib/src/provider/openai/`
- [ ] All unit tests under `packages/exploration_agent/test/provider/openai/` pass via `dart test packages/exploration_agent/test/provider/openai/`

title: Plugin authoring guide
description: v1 documentation deliverable per PRD §19. Covers: the plugin contract with worked examples, the three reference plugins as readable source, conventions (namespace selection, observation budget management, when to report busy, plugin-owned identifier surfacing), anti-patterns (subclassing the binding, hogging frame callbacks, unbounded fragments, swallowing exceptions), and the §20.2 taxonomy (configuration / composition / subclass / reimplementation plugins) for wrapping existing tools. Includes the extension/exploration/config.yaml manifest convention. The guide can be drafted from the contract alone; reference-plugin worked examples are slotted in as those plugins land.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `docs/plugin_authoring_guide.md` exists and is the single canonical guide file (referenced by stable URL from AGENTS.md template lenny-cx6.20 and DevTools panel lenny-cx6.21)
- [ ] Guide has a top-level section walking through the §7 contract (`ExplorationPlugin`, `ExplorationTool`, `ObservationContext`, `ExecutedAction`, `ToolResult`, `BusyState`, `JsonSchema`, `PluginContext`) with a worked inline example for each interface and value type — no host source needed to understand any of them
- [ ] Guide includes a complete end-to-end "hello world" plugin (namespace, one tool, observation fragment, busy-state, dispose) that compiles against `package:exploration_flutter/contract.dart` alone
- [ ] Guide has a "Reference plugins" section with three subsections (Router, Riverpod, Dio) each pointing at the canonical package path; subsections may be marked `(slotted in when lenny-cx6.25/26/27 lands)` with a TODO link back to this bead until the corresponding plugin merges
- [ ] Guide has a "Conventions" section covering: namespace regex `^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$` and uniqueness, observation budget (default 1KB serialized per plugin, return null when nothing relevant, truncate gracefully), when to report `BusyState.isBusy=true` (only plugin-known async work the host cannot see — frame work and animations are already host-covered), and the §21 plugin-owned identifier convention (each plugin's observation fragment includes the IDs its tools accept)
- [ ] Guide has an "Anti-patterns" section explicitly naming and explaining: subclassing `ExplorationBinding`, hogging `registerFrameCallback`, returning unbounded observation fragments, and swallowing exceptions inside `observe()`/`busyState()`
- [ ] Guide has a "Wrapping existing tools" section explaining the §20.2 taxonomy with a worked example for each of the four categories (configuration, composition, subclass, reimplementation); subclass example uses Marionette as the case study per §20.1
- [ ] Guide documents the §7.6 `extension/exploration/config.yaml` manifest convention with a complete sample manifest (namespace, Dart class, constructor requirements) and notes the alignment with `extension/mcp/config.yaml` (Packaged AI Assets)
- [ ] Guide has a "Versioning" section quoting the §7.7 posture: adding tools, expanding observation fragments, and refining busy-state heuristics are NON-breaking; release as often as you want
- [ ] Guide has a "Custom-widget plugins" subsection recommending a custom-widget plugin for apps with sparse semantics on custom design systems, and is the destination URL the host diagnostic from lenny-cx6.10 points users at
- [ ] Every PRD reference in the guide is a `§N.M` citation pointing at `docs/flutter_exploration_agent_prd_v0.5.md` (no duplicated PRD prose, no broken links)
- [ ] Guide passes `markdownlint docs/plugin_authoring_guide.md` (or equivalent — broken links, malformed code fences, undefined references all fail)
- [ ] Every Dart code snippet in the guide is wrapped in a fenced ```dart block; copy-pasting the "hello world" snippet into a freshly-scaffolded plugin package compiles under `dart analyze` against `package:exploration_flutter/contract.dart`
